steps.title: Recall what y-intercept means
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where a graph crosses the y-axis, which is the point on the line with $x = 0$. So we read the $y$-coordinate of the line at $x = 0$ from the figure.

steps.title: Read the y-intercept from the figure
steps.content: From the figure, the line crosses the y-axis at $(0, -4)$, which matches Choice D — $(0,-4)$.
